meaning: n.白天举行的音乐会,妇女便装之一种
examples: Leicester Square is still the perfect place to catch an afternoon matinee followed by a cappucino.
B: Not unless you want the matinee.
Yes, Sir. We have a matinee every Wednesday and Saturday.
"My face cannot fit on the television screen, " the 63-year-old former matinee idol moans.
For the matrons of New York's Madison Avenue, Saturday afternoon at the auction house replaced the Broadway matinee.
